messages.content:
Based on the content and contributions of your paper, here is a drafted **Limitations** section suitable for an ACL submission. It addresses inference dynamics, hyperparameter sensitivity, scaling constraints, and the scope of generation versus understanding.

***

\section{Limitations}
\label{sec:limitations}

While SDAR-VL demonstrates that block-wise discrete diffusion is a competitive backbone for vision-language understanding, several limitations remain that merit future investigation:

\paragraph{Inference Latency and Optimization.} Although block-wise diffusion allows for parallel decoding within blocks, the generative process still requires iterative denoising steps (typically 12--64 steps per block). Consequently, for short sequences where the parallelization gain does not amortize the cost of multiple iterations, SDAR-VL may exhibit higher inference latency compared to highly optimized autoregressive models (e.g., those utilizing vLLM or FlashDecoding). While our work focuses on the training framework and understanding performance, developing specialized serving kernels and step-distillation techniques for block-wise diffusion remains an essential step for real-time deployment.

\paragraph{Hyperparameter Sensitivity.} Our proposed Progressive Beta Noise Curriculum (PBNC) introduces additional hyperparameters, specifically the concentration growth rate and the final sharpness $C_{\text{final}}$. As shown in our ablation study (Table~\ref{tab:ablation_4b}), performance on different tasks (e.g., reasoning vs. robustness) can vary depending on the sharpness of the curriculum. This suggests that achieving optimal performance across all domains may require task-specific tuning of the noise schedule, adding complexity compared to the relatively standard training recipes of autoregressive models.

\paragraph{Model Scaling and Compute Constraints.} Due to academic computational resource constraints, our experiments are limited to the 4B and 8B parameter scales. While we observe positive scaling trends between these two sizes, it remains to be verified whether the stability and efficiency gains of the SDAR-VL framework hold at the scales of 30B or 70B+ parameters, where diffusion-based loss scaling dynamics can become more unpredictable.

\paragraph{Scope of Generative Capabilities.} Our evaluation suite primarily focuses on vision-language \emph{understanding} and reasoning (VQA, math, multiple-choice). While we demonstrate long Chain-of-Thought generation capabilities, we have not extensively benchmarked the model on open-ended creative content generation (e.g., storytelling or email writing). The rigid block boundaries inherent to the architecture might introduce artifacts in highly fluid, long-form text generation, a trade-off that requires further study in pure generation contexts.

\section{Limitations}
\label{sec:limitations}
While SDAR-VL demonstrates that block-wise discrete diffusion is a competitive backbone for vision-language understanding, several limitations remain that merit future investigation:
\paragraph{Model Scaling and Compute Constraints.} Due to academic computational resource constraints, our experiments are limited to the 4B and 8B parameter scales. While we observe positive scaling trends between these two sizes, it remains to be verified whether the stability and efficiency gains of the SDAR-VL framework hold at the scales of 30B or 70B+ parameters, where diffusion-based loss scaling dynamics can become more unpredictable.｜

generates text, image, and audio tokens in a unified denoising process, finally fulfilling the promise of true
general-purpose generative models.
发挥 diffusion 的最大价值

Here is the refined **Limitations** section. It incorporates your existing point about scaling and adds a forward-looking paragraph about the unified generative potential of diffusion models, polishing your specific text to fit the academic tone of the paper.

***

\section{Limitations}
\label{sec:limitations}

While SDAR-VL demonstrates that block-wise discrete diffusion is a competitive backbone for vision-language understanding, several limitations remain that merit future investigation:

\paragraph{Model Scaling and Compute Constraints.} Due to academic computational resource constraints, our experiments are limited to the 4B and 8B parameter scales. While we observe positive scaling trends between these two sizes, it remains to be verified whether the stability and efficiency gains of the SDAR-VL framework hold at the scales of 30B or 70B+ parameters, where diffusion-based loss scaling dynamics can become more complex and unpredictable.

\paragraph{Scope of Generality and Unified Generation.} The fundamental advantage of diffusion models lies in their modeling universality—the ability to handle continuous and discrete modalities within a single framework. However, due to scope and resource considerations, this work prioritizes solving the fundamental bottlenecks of training efficiency and stability for block-wise diffusion, restricting our evaluation to multimodal \emph{understanding} (i.e., text generation). Consequently, we have not yet exploited the model's capacity for generating visual or auditory content. We believe the next frontier will likely be Multimodal Native Diffusion, where a single diffusion transformer generates text, image, and audio tokens in a unified denoising process, finally fulfilling the promise of true general-purpose generative models.
